skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations.
skill.summary: Generate AI images with 50+ models including FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream via the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 39 external_commands patterns and 8 network URLs, but all are FALSE POSITIVE. The external_commands are documentation examples (backtick code blocks) showing CLI usage, not actual code execution. All network URLs point to the legitimate inference.sh service documentation. No malicious patterns exist. The skill safely wraps a CLI tool for AI image generation.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: All detections are Markdown code blocks (documentation examples) showing CLI commands for users. No actual shell execution in skill code.
URLs point to legitimate inference.sh documentation and resources. This is the official service the CLI connects to.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and authenticated. Users must have valid credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear dependency on external CLI tool that requires authentication - documented requirement, not a vulnerability.

content.value_statement: Creating high-quality AI images often requires multiple tools and subscriptions. This skill provides unified access to 50+ models including FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream through a single CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models
Support for FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve
Image-to-image transformation and editing with Reve
Aspect ratio control and multiple output formats
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Cannot generate images directly - depends on external API service
Image generation costs are billed through inference.sh platform
Some models may have rate limits or credit requirements

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate custom product mockups, social media graphics, and marketing visuals without design expertise.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Prototyping
use_cases.description: Rapidly visualize ideas for games, films, or products with AI-generated concept art and illustrations.

use_cases.title: Personal AI Art Projects
use_cases.description: Create unique AI artwork, avatars, and illustrations for personal use or social media profiles.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a cat astronaut floating in space with stars and planets visible in the background

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of a coffee mug on a wooden table with soft studio lighting and shallow depth of field

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic portrait of a woman at golden hour, warm sunlight, dramatic shadows, 4K quality, film photography style

prompt_templates.title: Text Rendering Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a vintage travel poster that clearly displays the text SWISS ALPS in elegant typography with mountain scenery

output_examples.input: Generate a sunset landscape with mountains and a lake
output_examples.output: Image URL returned from inference.sh API showing a photorealistic sunset scene with mountain silhouettes reflected in calm lake waters

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk city at night with neon lights in 16:9 aspect ratio
output_examples.output: Wide-format image of a futuristic cityscape with glowing neon signs, flying vehicles, and rain-slicked streets in cyberpunk aesthetic

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with lighting and style details for better results
Start with faster models like FLUX Klein for prototyping before using premium models
Include aspect ratio specifications when you need particular dimensions
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like make it pretty without describing the actual content
Avoid requesting copyrighted characters or brand logos you do not have rights to use
Do not expect photorealistic faces at small resolutions use appropriate model settings

faq.question: How do I install the required inference.sh CLI tool?
faq.answer: Run npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools to install. Then authenticate with infsh login using your inference.sh account credentials.

faq.question: Which AI image model should I use for my project?
faq.answer: For speed and cost use FLUX Klein or P-Image. For highest quality use Seedream 4.5 or Gemini 3 Pro. For text rendering use Reve.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The Reve model supports image-to-image editing. Provide an existing image URL with editing instructions in your prompt.

faq.question: How are image generation costs calculated?
faq.answer: Costs depend on the model and resolution. FLUX Klein costs approximately $0.0001 per image. Premium models like Seedream cost more. Check inference.sh pricing for current rates.

faq.question: Can I upscale low-resolution images?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the Topaz image upscaler by running infsh app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler with your image URL to enhance resolution.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for image output?
faq.answer: Images are returned as URLs to PNG or JPEG files hosted by inference.sh. You can download them from the provided URLs.
